title: betula alba var. hornemannii
intro: Betula alba var. hornemannii, also known as Hornemann's white birch, is a variety of the white birch tree (Betula alba) that is native to Russia and Eastern Europe. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Hornemann's white birch include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, making it a popular remedy for arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bark is typically prepared as a decoction, tea, or salve, and can also be used in herbal creams and ointments to soothe and calm the skin.
